entries.summary: Stores archival or seal-reference material without becoming live proof-primary or runtime-primary.
Issues or records authority objects, authority digests, and authority verification references.
Defines law, schemas, invariants, severity rules, and projection contracts.
Routes and explains documentation without replacing constitutional law, accepted state, or proof publication.
Holds evidence-root current-state, artifact index, and consistency surfaces subordinate to law and accepted epoch.
Defines or enforces governed-repository membership, review discipline, and branch-policy expectations without becoming constitutional law itself.
Accepts or filters human submissions into structured reviewable signal without absorbing jobs, proof, or runtime semantics.
Publishes proof or proof-linked material derived from already-defined truth surfaces.
Collects, normalizes, evaluates, repairs, quarantines, and emits epoch candidates.
Represents a bounded reference surface that explains or exposes already-defined truth without becoming the source of that truth.
Owns the primary public root entry surface and links adjacent canonical surfaces without collapsing their roles.
Executes governed runtime or reconciliation behavior and emits receipts, outputs, or candidate state.
Publishes component health, incidents, and maintenance state for live surfaces.
Represents an operator or user-facing tool surface with bounded executable or interactive function.
Provides verifier-facing interfaces that evaluate artifacts or certificates against already-defined rules.
Stores accepted graph, epoch, trust, contradiction, and history outputs.
